front: Author: kills an elephant 'solely to avoid looking a fool' while stationed as a colonial police officer in Moulmein, Burma, using a .44 Winchester
Author: rails against 'dying metaphors,' 'pretentious diction,' and 'meaningless words'; satirically translates Ecclesiastes into bad modern prose; offers 6 rules for writing
Author: defends Henry Miller's Tropic of Cancer using a Jonah metaphor; surveys 1930s literature and writers attracted to communism
Author: narrates the 'Ram! Ram! Ram!' cry of a Hindu about to be hanged; reflects 'never tell me that the dead look peaceful'
Novel: Winston Smith receives a note saying 'I love you' from Julia; tortured with rats in Room 101 by O'Brien; betrayed by Mr. Charrington
Novel: Emmanuel Goldstein authored The Theory and Practice of Oligarchical Collectivism; coined doublethink, Newspeak, and Big Brother
Author: fought for the POUM faction of the Republican army in the Spanish Civil War; shot through the neck by a sniper; spent three days atop the Poliorama theater during the May Days
Novel: Gordon Comstock works at the New Albion advertising agency while writing the poem 'London Pleasures'; Rosemary Waterlow confesses she is pregnant
Author: describes the bleak conditions of coal miners in northern England; second section analyzes why not all people are socialists
back: The Road to Wigan Pier (George Orwell)
